name: CS Fundamentals
description: Algorithms, data structures, OS concepts, systems design

questions.question: What is time complexity of binary search?
questions.explanation: Binary search halves search space each iteration: O(log n).

questions.question: What data structure uses LIFO?
questions.explanation: Stack is Last In First Out - like a stack of plates.

questions.question: What is a hash table collision?
options: Two different keys hash to same index
questions.explanation: Collision occurs when hash function maps different keys to same bucket.

questions.question: What is Big O notation?
options: Upper bound on growth rate of algorithm
questions.explanation: Big O describes worst-case asymptotic time/space complexity.

questions.question: What is a linked list?
options: Sequence of nodes where each points to next
questions.explanation: Linked list: nodes contain data and pointer to next node.

questions.question: What is recursion?
questions.explanation: Recursion: function solves problem by calling itself with smaller input.

questions.question: What is a binary tree?
options: Tree where each node has at most 2 children
questions.explanation: Binary tree: each node has left and right child (or null).

questions.question: What does DFS stand for?
questions.explanation: DFS explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.

questions.question: What is a queue?
questions.explanation: Queue is First In First Out - like a line at a store.

questions.question: What is sorting stability?
questions.explanation: Stable sort preserves original order of equal elements.

questions.question: What is a graph?
options: Set of nodes connected by edges
questions.explanation: Graph: nodes (vertices) connected by edges, modeling relationships.

questions.question: What is an array?
options: Contiguous block of memory storing elements of same type
questions.explanation: Array: fixed-size sequential collection with O(1) index access.

questions.question: What is a pointer?
options: Variable storing memory address of another variable
questions.explanation: Pointer holds memory address, enabling indirect data access.

questions.question: What is quicksort average case?
questions.explanation: Quicksort average case: O(n log n). Worst case (sorted): O(n²).

questions.question: What is memoization?
options: Caching results of expensive function calls
questions.explanation: Memoization stores function results to avoid redundant computation.

questions.question: What is BFS?
questions.explanation: BFS explores all neighbors at current depth before going deeper.

questions.question: What is a heap?
options: Complete binary tree satisfying heap property (parent ≥ children)
questions.explanation: Heap: tree-based structure for priority queue with O(log n) operations.

questions.question: What is dynamic programming?
options: Breaking problem into overlapping subproblems and caching solutions
questions.explanation: DP solves problems by breaking into subproblems and reusing solutions.

questions.question: What is a BST?
options: Binary Search Tree where left < parent < right
questions.explanation: BST maintains sorted order: left subtree < node < right subtree.

questions.question: What is space complexity?
questions.explanation: Space complexity: amount of memory algorithm uses relative to input size.

questions.question: What is a trie?
options: Tree for strings where each node represents a character
questions.explanation: Trie: stores strings efficiently for fast prefix searches.

questions.question: What is Dijkstras algorithm for?
options: Shortest path in weighted graph with non-negative weights
questions.explanation: Dijkstra finds shortest paths from source using priority queue.

questions.question: What is a deadlock?
options: Two or more processes waiting for each other to release resources
questions.explanation: Deadlock: circular wait where processes block each other indefinitely.

questions.question: What is virtual memory?
options: Using disk space as RAM extension to run larger programs
questions.explanation: Virtual memory: OS uses disk to extend RAM, swapping pages in/out.

questions.question: How does consistent hashing work?
options: Hash keys and nodes onto ring, assign each key to next clockwise node
questions.explanation: Consistent hashing: minimal key remapping when adding/removing nodes.

questions.question: What is the Byzantine Generals Problem?
options: Achieving consensus in distributed system with potentially malicious nodes
questions.explanation: Byzantine fault tolerance: consensus despite arbitrary failures/attacks.

questions.question: What is a Bloom filter?
options: Probabilistic data structure for set membership (false positives possible, no false negatives)
questions.explanation: Bloom filter: space-efficient, fast membership check with tunable false positive rate.

questions.question: What is the two-phase commit protocol?
options: Distributed transaction protocol ensuring atomic commits across nodes (prepare then commit)
questions.explanation: 2PC: coordinator ensures all participants prepare successfully before commit.

questions.question: What is eventual consistency?
options: Distributed system model where all replicas eventually converge if updates stop
questions.explanation: Eventual consistency: prioritizes availability over immediate consistency (BASE).
